name: Fried Chicken with Honey Mustard Vinaigrette
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine vinaigrette ingredients (honey, Dijon mustard, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper). Whisk in the oil until blended. Set aside.
2. Rinse the chicken pieces and pat dry. Slice diagonally into 1-inch wide strips.
3. To make the salad dressing, mix honey, Dijon mustard, and 1 tablespoon kosher salt and black pepper in a medium bowl. Add the chicken pieces and turn to coat. Cover and chill until ready to cook.
4. In a large skillet, cook pancetta over medium heat until crisp. Drain on paper towels, crumble, and set aside.
5. Wipe the skillet with paper towels. Combine the bread crumbs with the remaining salt, pepper, and pancetta in a small bowl.
6. Dip the coated chicken strips in buttermilk, then dredge in the bread crumb mixture, pressing firmly onto the chicken.
7. Pour enough oil into the wiped skillet to measure about 1/4 inch. Heat over medium-high heat until hot.
8. When the oil is hot, add the chicken and fry until golden on both sides and firm to touch, adjusting heat as necessary. Drain on paper towels and keep warm.
9. In a large bowl, toss the greens with vinaigrette. Divide evenly among 4 serving plates. Arrange the chicken on the greens and sprinkle with crumbled pancetta.
